blocks.title: 📘 MUST / MIGHT / CAN'T + SUAVIZAR
blocks.intro: Dos herramientas de B1: los modales de deducción (must / might / can't) para decir qué tan seguro estás, y las frases suavizantes para dar opiniones sin sonar agresivo.

cells.body:
Casi seguro que SÍ:
The server must be overloaded — look at these numbers.

Posible, no seguro:
It might be a caching problem.

Casi seguro que NO:
It can't be the design — we tested it twice.

I'd suggest…
It might be worth + -ing
Have you considered…?

You must change it. (orden)
It might be worth changing it. (sugerencia)

blocks.ladderLabel: Escala de certeza — de seguro que no a seguro que sí:
blocks.note: Cómo funciona: esta lección mezcla flashcards 🃏, escucha 🎧, completar 📝 y ordenar 🧩. Haz el ✓ CHECK en cada ejercicio.

blocks.title: ★ THE DESIGN DEBATE
blocks.intro: Las ventas de GreenMarket bajaron y Priya cree que el problema es el nuevo diseño de Marcos. Él no está de acuerdo — y tiene datos. ¿Puede defender su trabajo sin quemar la relación?

blocks.title: FRASES SUAVIZANTES · toca para girar
blocks.intro: El mismo mensaje, dicho con tacto. Aprende estas frases — las vas a escuchar en el audio.

blocks.title: AUDIO 1 · THE DEBATE
blocks.desc: Priya culpa al diseño por la caída de ventas. Marcos discrepa — con datos y con tacto. ¿Quién tiene razón?

questions.q: What does Priya think is the problem?
According to Marcos, what might the real problem be?
How does Marcos know it can't be the design?
options: The data shows people leave at checkout, not the home page

questions.q: They agree to test the checkout page speed for one week.

speaking.prompt: How did Marcos push back politely? Quote one softening phrase and one deduction (must/might/can't).
speaking.starter: He said 'I see it differently' and 'it can't be the design, because…'. Then he suggested…

transcript.html: Marcos, I've seen the numbers. Sales are down fifteen percent since the redesign. It must be the new design. We should go back to the old one.
I take your point, Priya — the timing looks bad. But I see it differently. Can we look at the evidence together?
The data shows people love the new home page — they stay longer than before. But they leave at the checkout page. So it can't be the design. Something else is wrong.
I'm not one hundred percent sure. It might be the new payment system — the checkout could be loading too slowly on phones. Slow pages kill sales.
That's possible. But we can't wait forever.
Agreed. So I'd suggest a one-week test: we measure the checkout speed, and it might be worth trying a faster payment option too. If I'm wrong, we go back to the old design. Fair?
Okay, Marcos. One week. Show me the data next Friday. And… good pushback. I like it when people bring evidence.

blocks.title: MUST, MIGHT o CAN'T
blocks.intro: Elige el modal correcto según la certeza.

items.q: Sales dropped the day we changed servers. It ___ be a coincidence!
The office lights are on and I hear voices. Someone ___ be working late.
I'm not sure why it's slow — it ___ be the wifi.
He answered in perfect French. He ___ be a beginner.
It ___ be worth testing the page on a phone.
She's not answering. She ___ be in a meeting.

blocks.title: ORDENA LA FRASE DIPLOMÁTICA
blocks.intro: Toca las palabras para formar la sugerencia suave.

items.answer: It might be worth testing the speed
Have you considered a faster option
I take your point but I see it differently

blocks.title: AUDIO 2 · ONE WEEK LATER
blocks.desc: Viernes siguiente: Priya le deja un mensaje a Marcos con el resultado del test. ¿Quién tenía razón?

options: The checkout was loading nine seconds on phones

questions.q: What happened to sales after the fix?

speaking.prompt: You are Marcos. Reply to Priya's message in 2–3 sentences — be professional, don't say 'I told you so'!
speaking.starter: Hi Priya, thanks for the update. I'm glad the test helped — I'd suggest we also…

transcript.html: Hi Marcos, it's Priya. It's Friday, so — the results. Well… you were right. It wasn't the design. The test showed the checkout page was taking nine seconds to load on phones. Nine! No wonder people were leaving. The team switched to the faster payment option on Tuesday, and sales have already recovered. So, I owe you one. Thank you for pushing back — and for doing it with data, not drama. That's exactly how I like to work. Enjoy your weekend, Marcos. You've earned it.

blocks.title: ✍️ DISAGREE, BUT NICELY
blocks.intro: Tu jefe/a propone algo que te parece un error (elige la situación). Escribe tu respuesta (5–7 frases):
checklist: Reconoce su punto primero (I take your point…)
Marca tu desacuerdo con tacto (I see it differently / With respect,…)
Usa un modal de deducción (must / might / can't be)
Propón un paso siguiente (I'd suggest… / It might be worth…)
blocks.placeholder: Hi …, I take your point about…
blocks.example: Hi (name), I take your point about (topic) — it's important. But I see it differently. If we (action), (possible problem), and that could (consequence). The data suggests (evidence). I'd suggest we (alternative) instead — it might be worth (waiting / testing). What do you think? Best, (your name)
blocks.hw: Escribe tu respuesta y grábate leyéndola con tono calmado y profesional. Envíame texto y audio por WhatsApp.
